perturbation.original_text: The terms of this section “Use of Evaluation Materials” shall remain in effect for 2 years from the effective date of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The terms of this section “Use of Evaluation Materials” shall remain in effect for 6 months from the effective date of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Limiting the restriction on the use of evaluation materials to six months may be insufficient to protect against misuse of confidential information, especially regarding securities law compliance.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Securities Exchange Act of 1934 Section 10(b) and Rule 10b-5
perturbation.law_explanation: Insider trading restrictions under Section 10(b) and Rule 10b-5 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 prohibit the use of material non-public information for trading purposes. While not directly specifying a timeframe, the duty not to trade on or disclose such information persists until the information becomes public. A six-month restriction on the use of evaluation materials does not guarantee compliance with these insider trading rules beyond that period, if the information remains non-public and material.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: 17 CFR § 240.10b-5 - Employment of manipulative and deceptive devices. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 240.10b-5 - Employment of manipulative and deceptive devices. CFR prev | next § 240.10b-5 Employment of manipulative and deceptive devices. It shall be unlawful for any person, directly or indirectly, by the use of any means or instrumentality of interstate commerce, or of the mails or of any facility of any national securities exchange , (a) To employ any device, scheme, or artifice to defraud, (b) To make any untrue statement of a material fact or to omit to state a material fact necessary in order to make the statements made, in the light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading, or (c) To engage in any act, practice, or course of business which operates or would operate as a fraud or deceit upon any person, in connection with the purchase or sale of any security . (Sec. 10; 48 Stat. 891 ; 15 U.S.C. 78j ) [ 13 FR 8183 , Dec. 22, 1948, as amended at 16 FR 7928 , Aug. 11, 1951] CFR Toolbox Law about... Articles from Wex Table of Popular Names Parallel Table of Authorities
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a legal contradiction because it reduces the time frame for restrictions on using evaluation materials from 2 years to 6 months. The Perturbation Explanation states that this might be insufficient to prevent misuse of confidential information, particularly regarding securities law compliance. The Law Explanation further details that insider trading restrictions under Section 10(b) and Rule 10b-5 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 persist until information becomes public, suggesting that a 6-month restriction may not be compliant. Scraped Law Snippet 1, citing 17 CFR § 240.10b-5, prohibits employing any device to defraud in connection with the purchase or sale of a security. The reduced timeframe could allow for the misuse of non-public information after the 6-month period but before the information becomes public, thus potentially violating securities laws.

perturbation.original_text: Without the Company’s prior written consent, neither you nor any of your affiliates will for a period of two years from the date of this agreement directly or indirectly solicit for employment or employ any of the officers or employees of the Company (or any person whose activities are dedicated to the Company).
perturbation.changed_text: Without the Company’s prior written consent, neither you nor any of your affiliates will for a period of five years from the date of this agreement directly or indirectly solicit for employment or employ any of the officers or employees of the Company (or any person whose activities are dedicated to the Company).
perturbation.explanation: Extending the non-solicitation period to five years could be deemed an unreasonable restraint of trade under California law, especially if it unduly restricts employees' opportunities.
perturbation.contradicted_law: California Business and Professions Code Section 16600
perturbation.law_explanation: California Business and Professions Code Section 16600 generally prohibits contracts that restrain a person from engaging in a lawful profession, trade, or business. While narrow restraints are sometimes permitted, a five-year non-solicitation agreement may be considered overly broad and an unreasonable restraint, especially if it significantly limits the former employees' ability to find new employment.
perturbation.location: Section No Unauthorized Contact or Solicitation
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, BPC 16600 skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Business and Professions Code - BPC DIVISION 7. GENERAL BUSINESS REGULATIONS [16000 - 18107] ( Division 7 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 61. ) PART 2. PRESERVATION AND REGULATION OF COMPETITION [16600 - 17365] ( Part 2 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 526. ) CHAPTER 1. Contracts in Restraint of Trade [16600 - 16607] ( Chapter 1 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 526. ) 16600. (a) Except as provided in this chapter, every contract by which anyone is restrained from engaging in a lawful profession, trade, or business of any kind is to that extent void. (b) (1) This section shall be read broadly, in accordance with Edwards v. Arthur Andersen LLP (2008) 44 Cal.4th 937, to void the application of any noncompete agreement in an employment context, or any noncompete clause in an employment contract, no matter how narrowly tailored, that does not satisfy an exception in this chapter. (2) This subdivision does not constitute a change in, but is declaratory of, existing law. (c) This section shall not be limited to contracts where the person being restrained from engaging in a lawful profession, trade, or business is a party to the contract. (Amended by Stats. 2023, Ch. 828, Sec. 1. (AB 1076) Effective January 1, 2024.)
perturbation.justification: The changed text extends the non-solicitation period from two years to five years. The Law Explanation states that a five-year non-solicitation agreement may be considered overly broad and an unreasonable restraint of trade, potentially violating California Business and Professions Code Section 16600. Scraped Law Snippet 1, referencing BPC 16600(a), states that every contract restraining someone from engaging in a lawful profession, trade, or business is void, except as provided in the chapter. The Perturbation Explanation indicates the extended period could be an unreasonable restraint of trade under California law. Therefore, the modification introduces a term that may violate California law, creating a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: You irrevocably and unconditionally agree to submit to personal jurisdiction and service and venue exclusively in any federal or state court within the State of Illinois having subject matter jurisdiction over any action, suit or proceeding arising out of or relating to this agreement and the rights and obligations contained herein and waive any objection you may have to such jurisdiction, service and venue.
perturbation.changed_text: You irrevocably and unconditionally agree to submit to personal jurisdiction and service and venue exclusively in any court within Cook County, Illinois, regardless of subject matter jurisdiction, over any action, suit or proceeding arising out of or relating to this agreement and the rights and obligations contained herein and waive any objection you may have to such jurisdiction, service and venue.
perturbation.explanation: Restricting jurisdiction to any court within Cook County, Illinois, regardless of subject matter jurisdiction, is a violation of fundamental jurisdictional principles.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Article III, Section 2 of the United States Constitution
perturbation.law_explanation: Article III, Section 2 of the United States Constitution limits the subject matter jurisdiction of federal courts. Similarly, state courts also have defined subject matter jurisdiction. A contract cannot override these constitutional and statutory limitations by stipulating that any court, regardless of its subject matter jurisdiction, has authority over a case. The modified text attempts to do just that, rendering the clause unenforceable.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Article III | U.S. Constitution | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Article III Primary tabs Section 1. The judicial power of the United States, shall be vested in one Supreme Court, and in such inferior courts as the Congress may from time to time ordain and establish. The judges, both of the supreme and inferior courts, shall hold their offices during good behaviour, and shall, at stated times, receive for their services, a compensation, which shall not be diminished during their continuance in office. Section 2. The judicial power shall extend to all cases, in law and equity, arising under this Constitution, the laws of the United States, and treaties made, or which shall be made, under their authority;--to all cases affecting ambassadors, other public ministers and consuls;--to all cases of admiralty and maritime jurisdiction;--to controversies to which the United States shall be a party;--to controversies between two or more states;-- between a state and citizens of another state ;--between citizens of different states;--between citizens of the same state claiming lands under grants of different states, and between a state, or the citizens thereof, and foreign states, citizens or subjects. In all cases affecting ambassadors, other public ministers and consuls, and those in which a state shall be party, the Supreme Court shall have original jurisdiction. In all the other cases before mentioned, the Supreme Court shall have appellate jurisdiction, both as to law and fact, with such exceptions, and under such regulations as the Congress shall make. The trial of all crimes, except in cases of impeachment, shall be by jury; and such trial shall be held in the state where the said crimes shall have been committed; but when not committed within any state, the trial shall be at such place or places as the Congress may by law have directed. Section 3. Treason against the United States, shall consist only in levying war against them, or in adhering to their enemies, giving them aid and comfort. No person shall be convicted of treason unless on the testimony of two witnesses to the same overt act, or on confession in open court. The Congress shall have power to declare the punishment of treason, but no attainder of treason shall work corruption of blood, or forfeiture except during the life of the person attainted. ‹ Article II up Article IV › U.S. Constitution Toolbox Explanation of the Constitution - from the Congressional Research Service
perturbation.justification: The modified text restricts jurisdiction to any court within Cook County, Illinois, regardless of subject matter jurisdiction. This directly contradicts fundamental jurisdictional principles because it attempts to grant authority to courts even when they lack the legal power to hear a case. The Law Explanation supports this, stating that constitutional and statutory limitations on subject matter jurisdiction cannot be overridden by contract. Scraped Law Snippet 2, Article III, Section 2 of the U.S. Constitution, establishes that the judicial power of the United States extends to specific cases, implying that not every court has jurisdiction over every case. Therefore, the modified text creates a legal contradiction because it attempts to expand jurisdiction beyond what is legally permissible, directly conflicting with established jurisdictional principles.
